reasoning: Based on im inital assumption that the baby has normal development, I was fairly confident in my answer. After the AIs interpretation of the childs symptoms confirming my assumed diagnoses, I saw now reason to change it. There is no sign of FTT, sleep apnea or lack of feeding. Overall seems like a normal developing child and I would educate the mother on the normal sleep patterns for a child of that age as well as what to look out for that would raise concern.

highlights.text: Awakens and cries at least once nightly, then returns to sleep after a bottle
≈14–15 hours—well within normal range for his developmental age.
No other concerning symptoms (no apnea, labored breathing, sweating, irritability out of proportion, failure to thrive, etc.).
Night wakings can increase around 6–12 months due to separation anxiety.

Conditions such as obstructive sleep apnea, reflux, or other medical disorders would typically have additional symptoms (snoring, gasping, poor weight gain, arching, frequent/prolonged awakenings), which are not present.
